```python
"""
Simple support-ticket REST API with FastAPI.

Features:
- User signup & login (JWT-based auth)
- Authenticated users can:
    - Create tickets (title, body, priority)
    - List their own tickets
- In-memory storage (for demo); replace with DB in real app.
"""

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import List, Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer, OAuth2PasswordRequestForm
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from jose import JWTError, jwt
from passlib.context import CryptContext

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Config
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

SECRET_KEY = "CHANGE_ME_TO_A_RANDOM_SECURE_STRING"
ALGORITHM = "HS256"
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 60

app = FastAPI(title="Support Ticket API")

oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="auth/token")
pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# In-memory data stores (replace with database in production)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

class UserInDB(BaseModel):
    id: int
    username: str
    hashed_password: str

fake_users_db: dict[str, UserInDB] = {}
next_user_id = 1

class Ticket(BaseModel):
    id: int
    owner_id: int
    title: str
    body: str
    priority: str
    created_at: datetime

fake_tickets_db: list[Ticket] = []
next_ticket_id = 1

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schemas
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

class UserCreate(BaseModel):
    username: str = Field(..., min_length=3, max_length=50)
    password: str = Field(..., min_length=6, max_length=100)

class UserPublic(BaseModel):
    id: int
    username: str

class Token(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str = "bearer"

class TokenData(BaseModel):
    username: Optional[str] = None

class TicketCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=200)
    body: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    priority: str = Field(..., regex="^(low|medium|high)$")

class TicketOut(BaseModel):
    id: int
    title: str
    body: str
    priority: str
    created_at: datetime

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Auth helpers
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_password_hash(password: str) -> str:
    return pwd_context.hash(password)

def verify_password(plain: str, hashed: str) -> bool:
    return pwd_context.verify(plain, hashed)

def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: Optional[timedelta] = None) -> str:
    to_encode = data.copy()
    expire = datetime.utcnow() + (expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES))
    to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)

def get_user_by_username(username: str) -> Optional[UserInDB]:
    return fake_users_db.get(username)

def authenticate_user(username: str, password: str) -> Optional[UserInDB]:
    user = get_user_by_username(username)
    if not user or not verify_password(password, user.hashed_password):
        return None
    return user

async def get_current_user(token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme)) -> UserInDB:
    credentials_exception = HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
        detail="Could not validate authentication credentials",
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
        username: Optional[str] = payload.get("sub")
        if username is None:
            raise credentials_exception
        token_data = TokenData(username=username)
    except JWTError:
        raise credentials_exception

    user = get_user_by_username(token_data.username)
    if user is None:
        raise credentials_exception
    return user

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Auth endpoints
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.post("/auth/signup", response_model=UserPublic, status_code=201)
def signup(user_in: UserCreate):
    global next_user_id
    if user_in.username in fake_users_db:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Username already registered")

    user = UserInDB(
        id=next_user_id,
        username=user_in.username,
        hashed_password=get_password_hash(user_in.password),
    )
    fake_users_db[user.username] = user
    next_user_id += 1
    return UserPublic(id=user.id, username=user.username)

@app.post("/auth/token", response_model=Token)
def login_for_access_token(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends()):
    user = authenticate_user(form_data.username, form_data.password)
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Incorrect username or password",
            headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
        )
    token = create_access_token(data={"sub": user.username})
    return Token(access_token=token)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ticket endpoints
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.post("/tickets", response_model=TicketOut, status_code=201)
def create_ticket(
    ticket_in: TicketCreate,
    current_user: UserInDB = Depends(get_current_user),
):
    global next_ticket_id
    ticket = Ticket(
        id=next_ticket_id,
        owner_id=current_user.id,
        title=ticket_in.title,
        body=ticket_in.body,
        priority=ticket_in.priority,
        created_at=datetime.utcnow(),
    )
    fake_tickets_db.append(ticket)
    next_ticket_id += 1
    return TicketOut(**ticket.dict())

@app.get("/tickets", response_model=List[TicketOut])
def list_my_tickets(current_user: UserInDB = Depends(get_current_user)):
    user_tickets = [t for t in fake_tickets_db if t.owner_id == current_user.id]
    return [TicketOut(**t.dict()) for t in user_tickets]

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Example curl usage (for reference)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Signup:
# curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/signup \
#      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#      -d '{"username":"alice","password":"secret123"}'
#
# 2) Get token:
# curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/auth/token \
#      -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
#      -d "username=alice&password=secret123"
#
# 3) Create ticket:
# curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/tickets \
#      -H "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>" \
#      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
#      -d '{"title":"Problem","body":"It is broken","priority":"high"}'
#
# 4) List own tickets:
# curl -X GET http://localhost:8000/tickets \
#      -H "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>"
```

Run with:

```bash
pip install fastapi uvicorn python-jose passlib[bcrypt]
uvicorn main:app --reload
```

(where `main` is the filename without `.py` if you save it as `main.py`).

```python
"""
Support Ticket API using FastAPI.

Features:
- Authenticated users can create tickets and list their own tickets.
- Rate limiting and quotas per user.
- Enforced HTTPS/TLS assumption + HSTS via middleware/headers.
- Input validation with Pydantic.
- SQL injection-safe via SQLAlchemy ORM.
- Object-level authorization (users see only their own tickets).
- Structured JSON logging without secrets/PII.
- Basic tests using TestClient + in-memory SQLite.

NOTE: This is a single-file example. In a real project you'd split modules.
"""

import logging
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime
from typing import List, Optional, Tuple

from fastapi import (
    Depends,
    FastAPI,
    HTTPException,
    Request,
    Response,
    status,
    Header,
)
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer, HTTPAuthorizationCredentials
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict
from sqlalchemy import (
    Column,
    DateTime,
    Integer,
    String,
    Text,
    create_engine,
    ForeignKey,
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, relationship, sessionmaker, Session
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse
from starlette.testclient import TestClient

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CONFIG / DB SETUP
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./tickets.db"  # for demo; use Postgres/MySQL in prod

engine = create_engine(
    DATABASE_URL,
    connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if DATABASE_URL.startswith("sqlite") else {},
)
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)
Base = declarative_base()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MODELS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    # In a real system we'd have more fields (email, hash, etc); omitted on purpose
    username = Column(String(64), unique=True, index=True, nullable=False)

    tickets = relationship("Ticket", back_populates="owner")


class Ticket(Base):
    __tablename__ = "tickets"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    public_id = Column(String(36), unique=True, index=True, nullable=False)  # UUIDv4 string
    title = Column(String(200), nullable=False)
    body = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    priority = Column(String(16), nullable=False)  # "low", "medium", "high"
    created_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow)
    owner_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)

    owner = relationship("User", back_populates="tickets")


Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# STRUCTURED LOGGING (JSON, REDACTION)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class RedactingJSONFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    """
    Very small JSON formatter with basic redaction and CR/LF stripping.
    This avoids secrets/PII in logs for our simple demo.
    """

    SENSITIVE_KEYS = {"authorization", "cookie", "password", "token"}

    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        base = {
            "ts": datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
            "level": record.levelname,
            "event": record.getMessage(),
        }

        # Attach extra fields if present
        for key, value in getattr(record, "__dict__", {}).items():
            if key.startswith("_"):
                continue
            if key in ("msg", "args", "exc_info", "exc_text", "stack_info", "levelname",
                       "levelno", "pathname", "filename", "module", "lineno", "funcName",
                       "created", "msecs", "relativeCreated", "thread", "threadName",
                       "processName", "process", "ts", "name"):
                continue
            if key.lower() in self.SENSITIVE_KEYS:
                continue
            if isinstance(value, str):
                value = value.replace("\r", "\\r").replace("\n", "\\n")
            base[key] = value

        # If an exception is present, record it (without stack locals)
        if record.exc_info:
            base["error"] = str(record.exc_info[1])

        # Very small JSON without external deps
        import json
        return json.dumps(base, ensure_ascii=False)


logger = logging.getLogger("ticket_api")
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
if not logger.handlers:
    handler = logging.StreamHandler()
    handler.setFormatter(RedactingJSONFormatter())
    logger.addHandler(handler)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECURITY / AUTHENTICATION
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

bearer_scheme = HTTPBearer(auto_error=False)

# For demo purposes we simulate a user DB keyed by token.
# DO NOT do this in production; integrate with real auth / session.
DEMO_TOKENS = {
    # token -> username
    "user1-demo-token": "alice",
    "user2-demo-token": "bob",
}


def get_db() -> Session:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def get_or_create_demo_user(db: Session, username: str) -> User:
    user = db.query(User).filter(User.username == username).first()
    if not user:
        user = User(username=username)
        db.add(user)
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(user)
    return user


async def get_current_user(
    credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(bearer_scheme),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
) -> User:
    """
    Very simple bearer-auth demo:
    - Expects header: Authorization: Bearer <token>
    - Maps token -> username via DEMO_TOKENS, then to DB user.
    - No secrets/PII logged.
    """
    if credentials is None or credentials.scheme.lower() != "bearer":
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="authentication_required",
        )

    token = credentials.credentials
    username = DEMO_TOKENS.get(token)
    if not username:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="invalid_token",
        )

    user = get_or_create_demo_user(db, username)
    return user


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RATE LIMITING / QUOTAS (per-user)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# In-memory store; replace with Redis in real deployments.
# Keys: (user_id, route_key) -> [timestamps...]
RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS: dict[Tuple[int, str], list[float]] = {}
TICKET_DAILY_COUNT: dict[int, Tuple[int, int]] = {}  # user_id -> (yyyyMMdd, count)

RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MINUTE = 60  # per route per user
CREATE_TICKET_DAILY_QUOTA = 100  # tickets per day per user


def check_rate_limit(user_id: int, route_key: str) -> None:
    """
    Simple sliding-window rate limit: N requests / minute per (user, route_key).
    """
    now = time.time()
    window = 60.0
    key = (user_id, route_key)
    timestamps = RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS.get(key, [])

    # drop old
    timestamps = [ts for ts in timestamps if now - ts < window]
    if len(timestamps) >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MINUTE:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="rate_limited",
        )

    timestamps.append(now)
    RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS[key] = timestamps


def check_ticket_quota(user_id: int) -> None:
    """
    Very simple daily ticket creation quota per user.
    """
    today = int(datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y%m%d"))
    day, count = TICKET_DAILY_COUNT.get(user_id, (today, 0))
    if day != today:
        day, count = today, 0
    if count >= CREATE_TICKET_DAILY_QUOTA:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="ticket_quota_exceeded",
        )
    TICKET_DAILY_COUNT[user_id] = (day, count + 1)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SCHEMAS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TicketCreate(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    title: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=200)
    body: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=5000)
    priority: str = Field(..., description="low | medium | high")

    def validate_priority(self) -> None:
        if self.priority not in {"low", "medium", "high"}:
            raise ValueError("invalid priority")


class TicketOut(BaseModel):
    id: str
    title: str
    body: str
    priority: str
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MIDDLEWARE: TLS / HSTS / STRUCTURED REQUEST LOGGING
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class SecurityMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    """
    - Enforce HTTPS-only assumption (rejects cleartext if detected via headers).
    - Adds HSTS header.
    - Emits one structured log event per request.
    """

    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        start_time = time.time()
        trace_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

        # Basic HTTPS enforcement heuristic:
        # In real deployments: rely on trusted proxy headers or run app behind TLS.
        scheme = request.headers.get("x-forwarded-proto", request.url.scheme)
        if scheme != "https":
            # Reject cleartext access; transport must be TLS-terminated before us.
            return JSONResponse(
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                content={
                    "error": "https_required",
                    "trace_id": trace_id,
                },
            )

        try:
            response: Response = await call_next(request)
        except Exception as e:
            # Global error handler (generic to client, detailed to log)
            logger.error(
                "unhandled_exception",
                extra={
                    "trace_id": trace_id,
                    "path": request.url.path,
                    "method": request.method,
                    "error": repr(e),
                },
            )
            return JSONResponse(
                status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
                content={
                    "error": "internal_error",
                    "trace_id": trace_id,
                },
            )

        duration_ms = int((start_time - request.state.get("start_time", start_time)) * 1000)
        route = request.scope.get("path", "")

        logger.info(
            "http_request",
            extra={
                "trace_id": trace_id,
                "http_method": request.method,
                "http_route": route,
                "status_code": response.status_code,
                "duration_ms": duration_ms,
            },
        )

        # HSTS header (assumes you serve over HTTPS externally)
        response.headers["Strict-Transport-Security"] = "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload"
        # Expose trace_id to client
        response.headers["X-Trace-Id"] = trace_id

        return response


class RequestStartMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    """
    Store request start time on request.state for better timing.
    """

    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        request.state.start_time = time.time()
        return await call_next(request)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# APP INITIALIZATION
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

app = FastAPI(title="Support Ticket API", version="1.0.0")

# CORS: allow only configured origins (set as needed)
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["https://app.example.com"],  # restrict to real frontend
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["GET", "POST"],
    allow_headers=["Authorization", "Content-Type"],
    expose_headers=["X-Trace-Id"],
)

app.add_middleware(RequestStartMiddleware)
app.add_middleware(SecurityMiddleware)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ROUTES
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.post(
    "/tickets",
    response_model=TicketOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
)
async def create_ticket(
    ticket_in: TicketCreate,
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """
    Create a support ticket for the authenticated user.

    Security:
    - Auth required (bearer demo).
    - Input validated (length, allowed priority).
    - Owner is always server-side set from current_user (no mass-assignment).
    - Rate limited & daily quota enforced per user.
    - Object stored via SQLAlchemy (no SQL injection).
    """
    # Semantic validation
    ticket_in.validate_priority()

    # Abuse controls
    check_rate_limit(current_user.id, "create_ticket")
    check_ticket_quota(current_user.id)

    # Create ticket
    public_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    ticket = Ticket(
        public_id=public_id,
        title=ticket_in.title,
        body=ticket_in.body,
        priority=ticket_in.priority,
        owner_id=current_user.id,
    )
    db.add(ticket)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(ticket)

    return TicketOut(
        id=ticket.public_id,
        title=ticket.title,
        body=ticket.body,
        priority=ticket.priority,
        created_at=ticket.created_at,
    )


@app.get(
    "/tickets",
    response_model=List[TicketOut],
)
async def list_tickets(
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """
    List tickets for the authenticated user.

    Security:
    - Auth required.
    - Object-level auth: query is filtered by owner_id in the DB.
    - Rate limited per user.
    """
    check_rate_limit(current_user.id, "list_tickets")

    tickets = (
        db.query(Ticket)
        .filter(Ticket.owner_id == current_user.id)
        .order_by(Ticket.created_at.desc())
        .all()
    )

    return [
        TicketOut(
            id=t.public_id,
            title=t.title,
            body=t.body,
            priority=t.priority,
            created_at=t.created_at,
        )
        for t in tickets
    ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BASIC TESTS (evidence of behavior)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_test_app() -> FastAPI:
    """
    Return the same app instance for tests.
    Uses the file-backed SQLite DB created earlier; for isolation in a larger
    project, you'd use a separate in-memory engine per test session.
    """
    return app


def run_tests() -> None:
    """
    Run a few basic tests using TestClient:
    - Enforces HTTPS requirement via x-forwarded-proto.
    - Auth required.
    - Ticket creation & listing per user.
    - Object-level isolation: user2 does not see user1's tickets.
    - Rate limit behavior (simple).
    """
    test_app = _make_test_app()
    client = TestClient(test_app)

    def auth_headers(token: str) -> dict:
        return {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
            "x-forwarded-proto": "https",
        }

    # 1) HTTP (no TLS) should be rejected
    r = client.get("/tickets")  # no x-forwarded-proto, default is http in TestClient
    assert r.status_code == 400
    assert r.json()["error"] == "https_required"

    # 2) Auth required
    r = client.get("/tickets", headers={"x-forwarded-proto": "https"})
    assert r.status_code == 401

    # 3) Create ticket as user1
    payload = {
        "title": "First issue",
        "body": "Something is broken.",
        "priority": "high",
    }
    r = client.post("/tickets", json=payload, headers=auth_headers("user1-demo-token"))
    assert r.status_code == 201
    data = r.json()
    assert data["title"] == payload["title"]
    assert data["priority"] == payload["priority"]
    ticket_id_user1 = data["id"]

    # 4) List tickets as user1
    r = client.get("/tickets", headers=auth_headers("user1-demo-token"))
    assert r.status_code == 200
    tickets_user1 = r.json()
    assert len(tickets_user1) >= 1
    assert any(t["id"] == ticket_id_user1 for t in tickets_user1)

    # 5) User2 cannot see user1's tickets
    r = client.get("/tickets", headers=auth_headers("user2-demo-token"))
    assert r.status_code == 200
    tickets_user2 = r.json()
    assert all(t["id"] != ticket_id_user1 for t in tickets_user2)

    # 6) Basic rate limit: exceed limit quickly for a single route
    # (we'll temporarily lower the limit for this test to hit it fast)
    global RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MINUTE
    old_limit = RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MINUTE
    RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MINUTE = 3
    try:
        for i in range(3):
            r = client.get("/tickets", headers=auth_headers("user1-demo-token"))
            assert r.status_code == 200
        r = client.get("/tickets", headers=auth_headers("user1-demo-token"))
        assert r.status_code == 429
    finally:
        RATE_LIMIT_MAX_PER_MINUTE = old_limit

    print("All tests passed.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Evidence: run tests when executed directly.
    run_tests()
    # To run the API server:
    #   uvicorn this_file:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
```

Below is a minimal example using **FastAPI** and **JWT-based authentication** to let authenticated users upload and store a profile picture.

Assumptions:
- You already have user accounts and a way to issue JWTs.
- `user_id` is inside the JWT’s payload.
- Images are stored on disk under `media/profile_pics/`, and the DB stores the filename/path.

---

### Directory structure (example)

```text
your_app/
  main.py
  auth.py
  models.py
  database.py
  media/
    profile_pics/
```

Create folders:

```bash
mkdir -p media/profile_pics
```

Install dependencies:

```bash
pip install fastapi uvicorn python-multipart pillow python-jose[cryptography] sqlalchemy
```

---

### `database.py` – simple SQLAlchemy setup

```python
# database.py
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, declarative_base

SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./test.db"  # use your own DB in real apps

engine = create_engine(
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}
)
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)
Base = declarative_base()


def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()
```

```python
# models.py
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String
from database import Base

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True, nullable=False)
```

```python
# auth.py
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer
from jose import JWTError, jwt
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from database import get_db
from models import User

# Use your own secure secret & algorithm
SECRET_KEY = "CHANGE_ME_TO_A_LONG_RANDOM_SECRET"
ALGORITHM = "HS256"

oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="token")  # /token endpoint not shown here


def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: Optional[timedelta] = None):
    to_encode = data.copy()
    if expires_delta:
        expire = datetime.utcnow() + expires_delta
    else:
        expire = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=15)
    to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)


def get_current_user(
    token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
) -> User:
    credentials_exception = HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
        detail="Could not validate credentials.",
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )

    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
        user_id: Optional[int] = payload.get("sub")
        if user_id is None:
            raise credentials_exception
    except JWTError:
        raise credentials_exception

    user = db.query(User).filter(User.id == user_id).first()
    if user is None:
        raise credentials_exception

    return user
```

```python
# main.py
import os
import imghdr
import uuid
from typing import Dict

from fastapi import FastAPI, UploadFile, File, Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from PIL import Image

from database import get_db
from models import User
from auth import get_current_user

app = FastAPI()

MEDIA_ROOT = "media"
PROFILE_PICS_DIR = os.path.join(MEDIA_ROOT, "profile_pics")
os.makedirs(PROFILE_PICS_DIR, exist_ok=True)


def validate_image(upload_file: UploadFile, max_size_mb: int = 5):
    # Basic size check (read bytes in memory; for very large files use streaming)
    contents = upload_file.file.read()
    size_mb = len(contents) / (1024 * 1024)
    if size_mb > max_size_mb:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail=f"Image too large. Max size is {max_size_mb} MB.",
        )

    # Check type via imghdr
    file_type = imghdr.what(None, h=contents)
    if file_type not in ("jpeg", "png", "gif", "bmp", "webp"):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Unsupported image type.",
        )

    # Optionally ensure it’s actually an image via Pillow
    try:
        Image.open(io.BytesIO(contents)).verify()
    except Exception:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Uploaded file is not a valid image.",
        )

    # Reset file pointer so we can write it later
    upload_file.file.seek(0)
    return file_type


@app.post("/users/me/profile-picture", response_model=Dict[str, str])
async def upload_profile_picture(
    file: UploadFile = File(...),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
):
    # Validate image
    import io  # local import since used only here

    try:
        file_type = validate_image(file)
    except HTTPException:
        # Reset file for safety
        file.file.close()
        raise

    # Build unique filename
    extension = {
        "jpeg": "jpg",
        "png": "png",
        "gif": "gif",
        "bmp": "bmp",
        "webp": "webp",
    }.get(file_type, file_type)

    filename = f"{current_user.id}_{uuid.uuid4().hex}.{extension}"
    file_path = os.path.join(PROFILE_PICS_DIR, filename)

    # Save to disk
    try:
        with open(file_path, "wb") as buffer:
            contents = await file.read()
            buffer.write(contents)
    finally:
        file.file.close()

    # Optionally delete old picture if it exists
    if current_user.profile_image_path:
        old_path = os.path.join(MEDIA_ROOT, current_user.profile_image_path)
        if os.path.exists(old_path):
            try:
                os.remove(old_path)
            except OSError:
                pass

    # Store relative path in DB
    relative_path = os.path.join("profile_pics", filename)
    current_user.profile_image_path = relative_path
    db.add(current_user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(current_user)

    return {
        "message": "Profile picture uploaded successfully.",
        "profile_image_url": f"/media/{relative_path}",
    }
```

## `app/logging_config.py`

```python
import logging
import sys
import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone

class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        base = {
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "level": record.levelname,
            "logger": record.name,
            "event": getattr(record, "event", record.getMessage()),
        }
        # Merge extra fields (excluding built-ins)
        for k, v in record.__dict__.items():
            if k in (
                "name", "msg", "args", "levelname", "levelno", "pathname",
                "filename", "module", "exc_info", "exc_text", "stack_info",
                "lineno", "funcName", "created", "msecs", "relativeCreated",
                "thread", "threadName", "processName", "process"
            ):
                continue
            if k == "event":
                continue
            base[k] = v
        if record.exc_info:
            base["exception"] = self.formatException(record.exc_info)
        return json.dumps(base, separators=(",", ":"))

def configure_logging() -> None:
    handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
    root = logging.getLogger()
    root.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    root.handlers = [handler]

    # Avoid logging secrets/PII by convention:
    # DO NOT log request bodies, cookies, Authorization headers, or emails.
```

## `app/rate_limit.py` (simple per-user in-memory rate limiting)

```python
import time
from collections import defaultdict, deque
from typing import Deque, Dict
from fastapi import HTTPException, status, Depends
from .config import settings
from .auth import User

# In-memory window per user; use Redis in production.
_buckets: Dict[str, Deque[float]] = defaultdict(deque)

def rate_limiter_upload(user: User = Depends()) -> None:
    now = time.time()
    window = 60.0
    max_requests = settings.upload_rate_limit_per_minute

    q = _buckets[user.id]
    # Drop timestamps older than 60s
    while q and now - q[0] > window:
        q.popleft()

    if len(q) >= max_requests:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="Upload rate limit exceeded",
        )
    q.append(now)
```

## `app/image_validation.py` (content-based validation and normalization)

```python
from io import BytesIO
from typing import Tuple
from PIL import Image, UnidentifiedImageError
from .config import settings

# Allowlist of output formats (normalize everything to one of these)
ALLOWED_FORMATS = {
    "JPEG": ".jpg",
    "PNG": ".png",
    "WEBP": ".webp",
}

def validate_and_normalize_image(data: bytes) -> Tuple[bytes, str]:
    """
    Validate an uploaded image (bytes) and normalize it by re-encoding.
    Returns (normalized_bytes, extension).
    """
    # Guard against huge bodies: we already capped total bytes at read time,
    # but enforce again here.
    if len(data) == 0 or len(data) > settings.max_upload_bytes:
        raise ValueError("Invalid image size")

    # Use Pillow to inspect without loading arbitrarily large images:
    try:
        img = Image.open(BytesIO(data))
        img.verify()  # quick structural check
    except (UnidentifiedImageError, OSError):
        raise ValueError("Unsupported image format")

    # Re-open because verify() leaves file in an unusable state
    img = Image.open(BytesIO(data))

    width, height = img.size
    if width <= 0 or height <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Invalid image dimensions")

    if width > settings.max_image_width or height > settings.max_image_height:
        raise ValueError("Image too large")

    fmt = (img.format or "").upper()
    if fmt not in ALLOWED_FORMATS:
        # normalize unknown-but-decodable formats to PNG
        fmt = "PNG"

    # Convert palette / mode to a safe target (RGB or RGBA)
    if img.mode not in ("RGB", "RGBA"):
        img = img.convert("RGBA")

    # Re-encode image to normalized bytes (strips metadata & polyglots)
    output = BytesIO()
    save_kwargs = {}
    if fmt == "JPEG":
        # JPEG has no alpha; convert appropriately
        if img.mode not in ("RGB",):
            img = img.convert("RGB")
        save_kwargs["quality"] = 85
        save_kwargs["optimize"] = True
    elif fmt == "PNG":
        save_kwargs["optimize"] = True

    img.save(output, format=fmt, **save_kwargs)
    normalized = output.getvalue()

    if len(normalized) == 0 or len(normalized) > settings.max_upload_bytes:
        raise ValueError("Normalized image too large")

    ext = ALLOWED_FORMATS[fmt]
    return normalized, ext
```

```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, UploadFile, File, Depends, HTTPException, status, Request
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pathlib import Path
import logging

from .config import settings
from .logging_config import configure_logging
from .auth import get_current_user, User
from .rate_limit import rate_limiter_upload
from .image_validation import validate_and_normalize_image
from .storage import generate_profile_image_path
from .models import UserProfile

configure_logging()
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

app = FastAPI()

# CORS: allow only your app origin(s). Do NOT use wildcard with credentials.
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["https://app.example.com"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["POST"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

@app.middleware("http")
async def add_logging_context(request: Request, call_next):
    # very simple per-request logging; in real systems, use trace_id from OTel
    path = request.url.path
    method = request.method
    try:
        response = await call_next(request)
        logger.info(
            "request_completed",
            extra={
                "event": "http_request",
                "http_request_method": method,
                "http_route": path,
                "http_status": response.status_code,
            },
        )
        return response
    except Exception:
        logger.exception(
            "request_failed",
            extra={
                "event": "http_request",
                "http_request_method": method,
                "http_route": path,
            },
        )
        raise

@app.post("/profile/picture", status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def upload_profile_picture(
    file: UploadFile = File(...),
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    _: None = Depends(rate_limiter_upload),
):
    """
    Authenticated endpoint to upload a profile picture.

    Security & correctness:
    - Auth required.
    - Rate-limited per user.
    - Body read with size cap.
    - Content-type detected from bytes with Pillow.
    - Allowed formats only; dimensions limited.
    - Re-encoded to normalized format to remove metadata/polyglots.
    - Stored under server-generated filename outside web root.
    """
    # Validate basic metadata (filename/Content-Type not trusted, but we can
    # enforce length limits here if desired)
    if file.filename and len(file.filename) > 255:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Filename too long",
        )

    # Read body with explicit size cap
    raw = await file.read(settings.max_upload_bytes + 1)
    if len(raw) == 0:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Empty file",
        )
    if len(raw) > settings.max_upload_bytes:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_413_REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE,
            detail="File too large",
        )

    try:
        normalized_bytes, ext = validate_and_normalize_image(raw)
    except ValueError as e:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail=str(e),
        )

    # Generate safe storage path and URL
    try:
        dest_path, public_url = generate_profile_image_path(user.id, ext)
    except RuntimeError:
        logger.error(
            "path_traversal_detected",
            extra={
                "event": "security_violation",
                "user_id": user.id,
            },
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Invalid path",
        )

    # Write file atomically
    dest_dir = dest_path.parent
    dest_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    tmp_path = dest_dir / (dest_path.name + ".tmp")

    try:
        with open(tmp_path, "wb") as f:
            f.write(normalized_bytes)
        tmp_path.replace(dest_path)
    except OSError:
        logger.exception(
            "file_write_failed",
            extra={
                "event": "storage_error",
                "user_id": user.id,
            },
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
            detail="Failed to store image",
        )

    # Persist URL to "DB"
    await UserProfile.set_profile_image(user.id, public_url)

    logger.info(
        "profile_picture_uploaded",
        extra={
            "event": "profile_picture_uploaded",
            "user_id": user.id,
            "file_ext": ext,
            "file_size": len(normalized_bytes),
        },
    )

    # Do not return filesystem path; only the public URL
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
        content={"profile_image_url": public_url},
    )
```

- This code assumes it is served behind a reverse proxy / load balancer that:
  - Terminates TLS (HTTPS) and enforces no HTTP plaintext.
  - Sets `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload`.
- Document this as an infra requirement and verify at deployment; it’s not repeated in code.

```python
    resp = client.post("/profile/picture", files=files, headers=_auth_headers())
    assert resp.status_code == 400
```

Below is a minimal but solid example of a background worker in Python that processes a queue of outbound transactional emails.

This example uses:

- `Redis` as the message queue
- `rq` (Redis Queue) as the worker framework
- `smtplib` for actually sending email (you can swap it for any provider’s API)

### 1. Install dependencies

```bash
pip install redis rq python-dotenv
```

### 2. Project structure

```text
email_worker_example/
├─ .env
├─ config.py
├─ mailer.py
├─ enqueue_email.py   # script to enqueue emails
└─ worker.py          # background worker
```

### 3. `.env` – configuration

```env
SMTP_HOST=smtp.example.com
SMTP_PORT=587
```

### 5. `mailer.py` – function that sends the actual email

```python
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from email.utils import formataddr

from config import SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASSWORD, SMTP_FROM


def send_transactional_email(to_email: str, subject: str, body: str) -> None:
    """
    Sends a transactional email using SMTP.
    Raise an exception on error so rq can mark the job as failed/retry.
    """

    msg = MIMEText(body, "plain", "utf-8")
    msg["Subject"] = subject
    msg["From"] = formataddr(("Your App", SMTP_FROM))
    msg["To"] = to_email

    # Set up the SMTP connection
    with smtplib.SMTP(SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT) as server:
        server.starttls()
        if SMTP_USER and SMTP_PASSWORD:
            server.login(SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASSWORD)
        server.send_message(msg)

    # If we reach here, email was (likely) sent successfully
    print(f"[send_transactional_email] Sent to {to_email}")
```

```python
from redis import Redis
from rq import Queue

from config import REDIS_URL
from mailer import send_transactional_email


def enqueue_email(to_email: str, subject: str, body: str):
    redis_conn = Redis.from_url(REDIS_URL)
    q = Queue("emails", connection=redis_conn)

        send_transactional_email,
        to_email,
        subject,
        body,
        job_timeout=60,         # seconds
        retry=3,                # automatic retries
    )

    print(f"Enqueued job {job.id} to send email to {to_email}")
```

```python
import os

from config import REDIS_URL

listen_queues = ["emails"]


def run_worker():
```

```python
import os

from dataclasses import dataclass

class ConfigError(Exception):
    pass


def required_env(name: str) -> str:
    val = os.getenv(name)
    if not val:
        raise ConfigError(f"Missing required environment variable {name}")
    return val


def required_secret(name: str) -> str:
    """
    Secret loader: supports FOO or FOO_FILE; fails fast, never logs value.
    """
    file_var = f"{name}_FILE"
    if file_path := os.getenv(file_var):
        p = Path(file_path)
        if not p.is_file():
            raise ConfigError(f"Secret file for {name} not found at {p}")
        val = p.read_text().strip()
        if not val:
            raise ConfigError(f"Secret {name} from file is empty")
        return val
    if val := os.getenv(name):
        return val
    raise ConfigError(f"Missing required secret {name}")


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SMTPConfig:
    host: str
    port: int
    username: Optional[str]
    password: Optional[str]
    use_tls: bool
    timeout_sec: float

    def create_ssl_context(self) -> ssl.SSLContext:
        # Enforce TLS, no plaintext fallback
        ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
        ctx.check_hostname = True
        ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_REQUIRED
        return ctx


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class WorkerConfig:
    max_concurrent_sends: int = 10
    max_queue_size: int = 1000
    per_recipient_rate_per_minute: int = 60

    max_retries: int = 2

    retry_cap_delay_sec: float = 5.0
    hard_shutdown_grace_sec: float = 30.0


def load_smtp_config() -> SMTPConfig:
    host = required_env("SMTP_HOST")
    port = int(os.getenv("SMTP_PORT", "587"))
    username = os.getenv("SMTP_USERNAME")
    password = os.getenv("SMTP_PASSWORD")
    if username and not password:

    use_tls = os.getenv("SMTP_USE_TLS", "true").lower() == "true"
    timeout_sec = float(os.getenv("SMTP_TIMEOUT_SEC", "10.0"))

        # We never allow plaintext in this worker
        raise ConfigError("SMTP_USE_TLS must be true; plaintext SMTP is not allowed")
    return SMTPConfig(
        host=host,
        port=port,
        username=username,
        password=password,
        use_tls=use_tls,
        timeout_sec=timeout_sec,
```

## `email_worker/logging_setup.py`

```python
import json
import logging
import os
import re
import sys
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Dict


_SECRET_KEY_PATTERN = re.compile(
    r"(password|passwd|secret|token|authorization|cookie|api[_-]?key|x-api-key)",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)
_SECRET_VALUE_PATTERN = re.compile(
    r"(Bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9_\-\.]+|eyJ[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{10,}\.[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+\.[A-Za-z0-9_\-]+)"
)


class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        event: Dict[str, Any] = {
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "level": record.levelname,
            "logger": record.name,
            "event": getattr(record, "event", record.getMessage() or record.msg),
        }

        # Standard fields
        if record.exc_info:
            event["exception.type"] = record.exc_info[0].__name__
            event["exception.message"] = str(record.exc_info[1])

        # Structured extra fields
        for k, v in record.__dict__.items():
            if k.startswith("_") or k in (
                "msg",
                "args",
                "levelname",
                "levelno",
                "pathname",
                "filename",
                "module",
                "exc_info",
                "exc_text",
                "stack_info",
                "lineno",
                "funcName",
                "created",
                "msecs",
                "relativeCreated",
                "thread",
                "threadName",
                "processName",
                "process",
                "name",
            ):
                continue
            safe_key = str(k)
            safe_val = self._scrub_value(v, safe_key)
            event[safe_key] = safe_val

        return json.dumps(event, separators=(",", ":"))

    @staticmethod
    def _scrub_value(v: Any, key: str) -> Any:
        if isinstance(v, str):
            if _SECRET_KEY_PATTERN.search(key) or _SECRET_VALUE_PATTERN.search(v):
                return "[REDACTED]"
            return v
        if isinstance(v, dict):
            return {
                k: JsonFormatter._scrub_value(val, k)
                for k, val in v.items()
            }
        return v


def configure_logging() -> None:
    level = os.getenv("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
    root = logging.getLogger()
    root.setLevel(level)
    handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream=sys.stdout)
    handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
    root.handlers = [handler]
```

```python
import threading
import time
from collections import defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, Tuple


@dataclass
class Counter:
    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock, init=False)
    _value: int = 0

    def inc(self, n: int = 1) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._value += n

    def get(self) -> int:
        with self._lock:
            return self._value


@dataclass
class Histogram:
    """
    Simple histogram storing sum and count; not for prod PromQL but enough
    to build tests and basic monitoring hooks.
    """
    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock, init=False)
    _count: int = 0
    _sum: float = 0.0

    def observe(self, value: float) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._count += 1
            self._sum += value

    def snapshot(self) -> Tuple[int, float]:
        with self._lock:
            return self._count, self._sum


class WorkerMetrics:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.emails_enqueued_total = Counter()
        self.emails_sent_total = Counter()
        self.emails_failed_total = Counter()
        self.emails_dropped_total = Counter()
        self.smtp_errors_total = Counter()
        self.email_send_duration_seconds = Histogram()
        self._last_reset = time.time()


METRICS = WorkerMetrics()
```

## `email_worker/rate_limiter.py`

```python
import threading
import time
from collections import deque
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Deque, Dict


@dataclass
class TokenBucket:
    """
    Simple per-key sliding-window rate limiter:
    allows up to `limit` events in a `window_sec` window.
    """
    limit: int
    window_sec: float
    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock, init=False)
    _events: Dict[str, Deque[float]] = field(default_factory=dict, init=False)

    def allow(self, key: str) -> bool:
        now = time.time()
        with self._lock:
            q = self._events.setdefault(key, deque())
            # Evict old timestamps
            cutoff = now - self.window_sec
            while q and q[0] < cutoff:
                q.popleft()
            if len(q) >= self.limit:
                return False
            q.append(now)
            return True
```

## `email_worker/smtp_client.py`

```python
import logging
import smtplib
import socket
import ssl
from email.message import EmailMessage as MIMEMessage
from typing import Optional

from .config import SMTPConfig

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SMTPTransientError(Exception):
    """Retryable SMTP error."""


class SMTPPermanentError(Exception):
    """Non-retryable SMTP error (4xx/5xx that we treat as fatal)."""


class EmailSMTPClient:
    """
    Thin async-compatible wrapper around smtplib with explicit timeout and TLS.
    Use from a threadpool if integrating into an async system.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: SMTPConfig):
        self._cfg = config

    def send_email(
        self,
        to_addr: str,
        subject: str,
        body: str,
        from_addr: Optional[str] = None,
        timeout_sec: Optional[float] = None,
    ) -> None:
        timeout = timeout_sec or self._cfg.timeout_sec
        msg = MIMEMessage()
        msg["To"] = to_addr
        msg["Subject"] = subject
        if from_addr:
            msg["From"] = from_addr
        msg.set_content(body)

        try:
            context = self._cfg.create_ssl_context()
            with smtplib.SMTP(
                host=self._cfg.host,
                port=self._cfg.port,
                timeout=timeout,
            ) as smtp:
                # STARTTLS: we already required TLS; if server does not support, fail.
                smtp.starttls(context=context)
                if self._cfg.username and self._cfg.password:
                    smtp.login(self._cfg.username, self._cfg.password)

                smtp.send_message(msg)
        except (smtplib.SMTPConnectError, smtplib.SMTPServerDisconnected, socket.timeout) as e:
            logger.warning(
                "smtp_transient_error",
                extra={"event": "smtp_transient_error", "error.type": type(e).__name__},
            )
            raise SMTPTransientError(str(e)) from e
        except smtplib.SMTPException as e:
            logger.error(
                "smtp_permanent_error",
                extra={"event": "smtp_permanent_error", "error.type": type(e).__name__},
            )
            raise SMTPPermanentError(str(e)) from e
        except Exception as e:
            # Treat as transient by default but log at ERROR
            logger.error(
                "smtp_unknown_error",
                extra={"event": "smtp_unknown_error", "error.type": type(e).__name__},
            )
            raise SMTPTransientError(str(e)) from e
```

## `email_worker/worker.py`

```python
import logging
import queue
import random
import signal
import threading
import time
from typing import Callable, Optional

from .config import SMTPConfig, WorkerConfig
from .logging_setup import configure_logging
from .metrics import METRICS
from .models import EmailMessage
from .rate_limiter import TokenBucket
from .smtp_client import EmailSMTPClient, SMTPPermanentError, SMTPTransientError

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class EmailQueueFull(Exception):
    pass


class EmailWorker:
    """
    Background worker that processes a bounded in-memory queue of EmailMessage.

    - Bounded queue with backpressure/drop + logging.
    - Per-recipient rate limiting (token bucket per minute).
    - Explicit timeout and bounded retries with jitter on SMTP calls.
    - Graceful shutdown with best-effort drain subject to per-message timeout.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        smtp_config: SMTPConfig,
        worker_config: WorkerConfig,
        *,
        clock: Callable[[], float] = time.time,
    ):
        self._smtp_client = EmailSMTPClient(smtp_config)
        self._cfg = worker_config
        self._clock = clock

        self._queue: "queue.Queue[EmailMessage]" = queue.Queue(
            maxsize=self._cfg.max_queue_size
        )
        self._threads: list[threading.Thread] = []
        self._stop_event = threading.Event()

        self._rate_limiter = TokenBucket(
            limit=self._cfg.per_recipient_rate_per_minute,
            window_sec=60.0,
        )

    def start(self) -> None:
        for i in range(self._cfg.max_concurrent_sends):
            t = threading.Thread(target=self._run_loop, name=f"email-worker-{i}", daemon=True)
            t.start()
            self._threads.append(t)
        logger.info(
            "worker_started",
            extra={
                "event": "worker_started",
                "concurrency": self._cfg.max_concurrent_sends,
                "queue_max_size": self._cfg.max_queue_size,
            },
        )

    def stop(self, timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
        """
        Initiate graceful shutdown. We:
        - Stop accepting new work externally.
        - Signal worker threads to stop when queue is empty or stop_event is set.
        - Wait for up to `timeout` seconds (or configured grace) for threads to join.
        """
        self._stop_event.set()
        deadline = self._clock() + (timeout or self._cfg.hard_shutdown_grace_sec)
        for t in self._threads:
            remaining = deadline - self._clock()
            if remaining <= 0:
                break
            t.join(timeout=remaining)
        logger.info(
            "worker_stopped",
            extra={"event": "worker_stopped", "remaining_queue_size": self._queue.qsize()},
        )

    def enqueue(self, msg: EmailMessage, *, block: bool = False, timeout: float = 0.0) -> None:
        """
        Enqueue an email for sending.
        - Bounded queue; on overflow, either block or drop with structured log.
        """
        try:
            self._queue.put(msg, block=block, timeout=timeout)
            METRICS.emails_enqueued_total.inc()
        except queue.Full:
            METRICS.emails_dropped_total.inc()
            logger.warning(
                "queue_full_drop",
                extra={
                    "event": "queue_full_drop",
                    "email_id": msg.id,
                    "to": msg.to,
                },
            )
            raise EmailQueueFull("Email queue is full")

    # Internal worker loop

    def _run_loop(self) -> None:
        while not self._stop_event.is_set():
            try:
                msg = self._queue.get(timeout=0.5)
            except queue.Empty:
                continue

            try:
                self._process_message(msg)
            finally:
                self._queue.task_done()

    def _process_message(self, msg: EmailMessage) -> None:
        start = self._clock()

        if not self._rate_limiter.allow(msg.to):
            METRICS.emails_dropped_total.inc()
            logger.warning(
                "rate_limit_exceeded",
                extra={
                    "event": "rate_limit_exceeded",
                    "email_id": msg.id,
                    "to": msg.to,
                },
            )
            return

        attempts = 0
        while True:
            attempts += 1
            try:
                parts = msg.as_smtp_parts()
                self._smtp_client.send_email(
                    to_addr=msg.to,
                    subject=parts["subject"],
                    body=parts["body"],
                    from_addr=parts.get("from_addr"),
                    timeout_sec=self._cfg.send_timeout_sec,
                )
                duration = self._clock() - start
                METRICS.emails_sent_total.inc()
                METRICS.email_send_duration_seconds.observe(duration)
                logger.info(
                    "email_send_completed",
                    extra={
                        "event": "email_send_completed",
                        "email_id": msg.id,
                        "to": msg.to,
                        "duration_sec": round(duration, 3),
                        "attempts": attempts,
                    },
                )
                return
            except SMTPPermanentError as e:
                METRICS.emails_failed_total.inc()
                logger.error(
                    "email_send_permanent_failure",
                    extra={
                        "event": "email_send_permanent_failure",
                        "email_id": msg.id,
                        "to": msg.to,
                        "error.type": type(e).__name__,
                    },
                )
                return
            except SMTPTransientError as e:
                METRICS.smtp_errors_total.inc()
                if attempts > self._cfg.max_retries:
                    METRICS.emails_failed_total.inc()
                    logger.error(
                        "email_send_retry_exhausted",
                        extra={
                            "event": "email_send_retry_exhausted",
                            "email_id": msg.id,
                            "to": msg.to,
                            "attempts": attempts - 1,
                            "error.type": type(e).__name__,
                        },
                    )
                    return
                # Exponential backoff with full jitter
                delay = random.uniform(
                    0.0,
                    min(
                        self._cfg.retry_cap_delay_sec,
                        self._cfg.retry_base_delay_sec * (2 ** (attempts - 1)),
                    ),
                )
                logger.warning(
                    "email_send_retry_scheduled",
                    extra={
                        "event": "email_send_retry_scheduled",
                        "email_id": msg.id,
                        "to": msg.to,
                        "attempts": attempts,
                        "delay_sec": round(delay, 3),
                    },
                )
                # Sleep is acceptable here: this is a dedicated worker thread
                time.sleep(delay)
            except Exception as e:
                # Unknown failure: treat as transient but log clearly; still bounded by max_retries.
                METRICS.smtp_errors_total.inc()
                if attempts > self._cfg.max_retries:
                    METRICS.emails_failed_total.inc()
                    logger.error(
                        "email_send_unknown_failure",
                        extra={
                            "event": "email_send_unknown_failure",
                            "email_id": msg.id,
                            "to": msg.to,
                            "attempts": attempts - 1,
                            "error.type": type(e).__name__,
                        },
                    )
                    return
                delay = random.uniform(
                    0.0,
                    min(
                        self._cfg.retry_cap_delay_sec,
                        self._cfg.retry_base_delay_sec * (2 ** (attempts - 1)),
                    ),
                )
                logger.warning(
                    "email_send_retry_scheduled_unknown",
                    extra={
                        "event": "email_send_retry_scheduled_unknown",
                        "email_id": msg.id,
                        "to": msg.to,
                        "attempts": attempts,
                        "delay_sec": round(delay, 3),
                    },
                )
                time.sleep(delay)


def install_signal_handlers(worker: EmailWorker) -> None:
    def _handler(signum, frame):
        logger.info(
            "signal_received",
            extra={"event": "signal_received", "signal": signum},
        )
        worker.stop()

    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handler)
    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)


def main() -> None:
    """
    Entrypoint for a standalone worker process.

    Usage:
      export SMTP_HOST=...
      export SMTP_USERNAME=... (optional)
      export SMTP_PASSWORD=... or SMTP_PASSWORD_FILE=...
      python -m email_worker.worker
    """
    configure_logging()
    from .config import load_smtp_config, load_worker_config

    smtp_cfg = load_smtp_config()
    worker_cfg = load_worker_config()

    worker = EmailWorker(smtp_cfg, worker_cfg)
    install_signal_handlers(worker)
    worker.start()

    # Block forever; signals trigger graceful shutdown.
    while True:
        time.sleep(60)
```

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordRequestForm
from jose import JWTError, jwt
from passlib.context import CryptContext
from pydantic import BaseModel

# =====================================
# Config
# =====================================

SECRET_KEY = "CHANGE_THIS_TO_A_LONG_RANDOM_SECRET"
ALGORITHM = "HS256"
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 60

# =====================================
# Models
# =====================================

class UserInDB(BaseModel):
    username: str
    hashed_password: str
    disabled: bool = False


class User(BaseModel):
    username: str
    disabled: bool = False


class Token(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str


# =====================================
# Fake in-memory "database"
# =====================================

pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")

# Pre-hash a password: `pwd_context.hash("password123")`
fake_users_db = {
    "alice": UserInDB(
        username="alice",
        hashed_password=pwd_context.hash("password123"),
        disabled=False,
    ),
    "bob": UserInDB(
        username="bob",
        hashed_password=pwd_context.hash("secret456"),
        disabled=False,
    ),
}


def get_user(username: str) -> Optional[UserInDB]:
    return fake_users_db.get(username)


def verify_password(plain_password: str, hashed_password: str) -> bool:
    return pwd_context.verify(plain_password, hashed_password)


def authenticate_user(username: str, password: str) -> Optional[UserInDB]:
    user = get_user(username)
    if not user:
        return None
    if not verify_password(password, user.hashed_password):
        return None
    if user.disabled:
        return None
    return user


def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: Optional[timedelta] = None) -> str:
    to_encode = data.copy()
    expire = datetime.utcnow() + (expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=15))
    to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)

# FastAPI app and endpoints
# =====================================

app = FastAPI()

async def login(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends()):
    """
    User login endpoint.

    Request (x-www-form-urlencoded):
      - username: string
      - password: string

    Response:
      - access_token: JWT
      - token_type: "bearer"
    """
    user = authenticate_user(form_data.username, form_data.password)
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(

            detail="Incorrect username or password",
            headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
        )

    access_token_expires = timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)
    access_token = create_access_token(
        data={"sub": user.username},
        expires_delta=access_token_expires,
    )
    return {"access_token": access_token, "token_type": "bearer"}

oauth2_scheme = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="login")


async def get_current_user(token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme)) -> User:
    credentials_exception = HTTPException(
        status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
        detail="Could not validate credentials",
        headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"},
    )

    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
        username: str = payload.get("sub")
        if username is None:
            raise credentials_exception
    except JWTError:
        raise credentials_exception

    user_in_db = get_user(username)
    if user_in_db is None:
        raise credentials_exception

    return User(username=user_in_db.username, disabled=user_in_db.disabled)


@app.get("/me", response_model=User)
async def read_me(current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)):
    return current_user
```

```python
Secure login endpoint for FastAPI.

Features:
- Argon2id password hashing with rehash-on-login
- Uniform error messages/timing (no account enumeration)
- Per-IP and per-username rate limiting
- Session management using secure HttpOnly cookies
- Optional "remember me" long-lived token with selector/validator pattern

- Basic tests (pytest) at bottom of file

import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

from argon2 import PasswordHasher, exceptions as argon2_exceptions
import logging

# Argon2id parameters tuned for ~0.2-0.5s on typical servers; adjust as needed.
ph = PasswordHasher(
    time_cost=3,
    memory_cost=65536,  # 64 MiB
    parallelism=4,
)

SESSION_COOKIE_NAME = "__Host-session"
REMEMBER_COOKIE_NAME = "__Host-remember"

SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT = timedelta(minutes=30)
SESSION_ABSOLUTE_TIMEOUT = timedelta(hours=12)

REMEMBER_TOKEN_LIFETIME = timedelta(days=30)

# Rate limiting: tune as needed.
LOGIN_PER_IP_LIMIT = 10  # attempts per 10 minutes
LOGIN_PER_IP_WINDOW = 600
LOGIN_PER_USER_LIMIT = 5  # attempts per 10 minutes
LOGIN_PER_USER_WINDOW = 600

# For demo only. In production, store secrets in KMS/secret manager.
REMEMBER_TOKEN_HMAC_KEY = secrets.token_bytes(32)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOGGING (structured, JSON-like)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

logger = logging.getLogger("auth")
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
handler = logging.StreamHandler()
formatter = logging.Formatter(
    fmt='{"event":"%(message)s","level":"%(levelname)s",'
        '"timestamp":"%(asctime)s","fields":%(fields)s}',
    datefmt="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z",
)


class RedactingAdapter(logging.LoggerAdapter):
    """
    Ensure we never log secrets, passwords, tokens, or raw request bodies.
    """

    def process(self, msg, kwargs):
        fields = kwargs.get("extra", {}) or {}
        scrubbed: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        for k, v in fields.items():
            key_l = k.lower()
            if any(s in key_l for s in ["password", "token", "secret", "cookie"]):
                scrubbed[k] = "[REDACTED]"
            else:
                scrubbed[k] = v
        # naive JSON-ish encoding to keep example short and dependency-free
        kwargs["extra"] = {"fields": str(scrubbed).replace('"', '\\"')}
        return msg, kwargs


handler.setFormatter(formatter)
logger.addHandler(handler)
slog = RedactingAdapter(logger, {})

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IN-MEMORY "DB" (REPLACE WITH REAL PERSISTENCE)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

class UserRecord(BaseModel):
    id: str
    email: EmailStr
    password_hash: str
    locked: bool = False
    mfa_enabled: bool = False  # Placeholder for future MFA


class SessionRecord(BaseModel):
    id: str
    user_id: str
    created_at: datetime
    last_seen_at: datetime


class RememberTokenRecord(BaseModel):
    selector: str
    validator_hash: str
    user_id: str
    expires_at: datetime


USERS: Dict[str, UserRecord] = {}          # key: email.lower()
SESSIONS: Dict[str, SessionRecord] = {}    # key: session_id
REMEMBER_TOKENS: Dict[str, RememberTokenRecord] = {}  # key: selector

# In-memory rate limit stores: key -> list[timestamps]
RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS: Dict[str, list[float]] = {}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MODELS
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

class LoginRequest(BaseModel):
    email: EmailStr
    password: str
    remember_me: bool = False

    @field_validator("password")
    @classmethod
    def min_length(cls, v: str) -> str:
        # For login, we do not enforce policy, just reject obviously empty.
        if len(v) == 0:
            raise ValueError("password required")
        return v


class LoginResponse(BaseModel):
    success: bool
    user_id: str | None = None  # non-sensitive user identifier
    # Add more non-PII fields as needed.


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPERS: PASSWORDS
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
    return ph.hash(password)


def verify_password(stored_hash: str, candidate: str) -> bool:
    try:
        if ph.verify(stored_hash, candidate):
            if ph.check_needs_rehash(stored_hash):
                # Caller must persist the new hash.
                slog.info(
                    "password_rehash_needed",
                    extra={"user_hash_prefix": stored_hash[:20]},
                )
            return True
        return False
    except argon2_exceptions.VerifyMismatchError:
        return False
    except argon2_exceptions.VerificationError:
        # Treat all errors as mismatch to avoid leaking info.
        return False


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPERS: RATE LIMITING (simple sliding window)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

def rate_limited(key: str, limit: int, window_seconds: int) -> bool:
    now = time.time()
    bucket = RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS.get(key, [])
    # Drop old entries
    bucket = [t for t in bucket if now - t < window_seconds]
    if len(bucket) >= limit:
        RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS[key] = bucket
        return True
    bucket.append(now)
    RATE_LIMIT_BUCKETS[key] = bucket
    return False


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HELPERS: SESSIONS & REMEMBER-ME TOKENS
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

def create_session(user_id: str) -> SessionRecord:
    sid = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)  # ≥256 bits entropy
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    session = SessionRecord(id=sid, user_id=user_id, created_at=now, last_seen_at=now)
    SESSIONS[sid] = session
    return session


def touch_session(session: SessionRecord) -> None:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    session.last_seen_at = now
    SESSIONS[session.id] = session


def is_session_expired(session: SessionRecord) -> bool:
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    if now - session.last_seen_at > SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT:
        return True
    if now - session.created_at > SESSION_ABSOLUTE_TIMEOUT:
        return True
    return False


def destroy_session(session_id: str) -> None:
    SESSIONS.pop(session_id, None)


def create_remember_token(user_id: str) -> RememberTokenRecord:
    # selector: non-secret index; validator: secret, hashed at rest.
    selector = secrets.token_urlsafe(16)
    validator = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
    validator_hash = hashlib.sha256(validator.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
    rec = RememberTokenRecord(
        selector=selector,
        validator_hash=validator_hash,
        user_id=user_id,
        expires_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) + REMEMBER_TOKEN_LIFETIME,
    )
    REMEMBER_TOKENS[selector] = rec
    return rec, validator  # caller sets cookie with selector:validator


def verify_remember_token(cookie_value: str) -> Optional[str]:
    try:
        selector, validator = cookie_value.split(":", 1)
    except ValueError:
        return None
    rec = REMEMBER_TOKENS.get(selector)
    if not rec:
        return None
    if datetime.now(timezone.utc) > rec.expires_at:
        REMEMBER_TOKENS.pop(selector, None)
        return None
    cand_hash = hashlib.sha256(validator.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
    if not hmac.compare_digest(cand_hash, rec.validator_hash):
        return None
    # Rotate token on use: single-use
    REMEMBER_TOKENS.pop(selector, None)
    new_rec, _ = create_remember_token(rec.user_id)
    # new validator will be returned to caller separately; this function
    # is for backend lookup only.
    return rec.user_id

@app.middleware("http")
async def request_logger(request: Request, call_next):
    start = time.time()
    trace_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    request.state.trace_id = trace_id

    # Extract IP best-effort; in real deployment, use X-Forwarded-For from trusted proxy
    client_ip = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"

    try:
        response = await call_next(request)
    except Exception as e:
        duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
        slog.error(
            "unhandled_exception",
            extra={
                "trace_id": trace_id,
                "path": request.url.path,
                "method": request.method,
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "duration_ms": duration_ms,
                "error_type": type(e).__name__,
            },
        )
        return JSONResponse(
            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
            content={"error": "internal_error", "trace_id": trace_id},
        )

    duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
    slog.info(
        "http_request",
        extra={
            "trace_id": trace_id,
            "path": request.url.path,
            "method": request.method,
            "status_code": response.status_code,
            "client_ip": client_ip,
            "duration_ms": duration_ms,
        },
    )
    # Propagate trace ID for clients
    response.headers["X-Request-Id"] = trace_id
    return response

def get_current_user_id_from_session(request: Request) -> Optional[str]:
    cookie = request.cookies.get(SESSION_COOKIE_NAME)
    if not cookie:
        # Optionally, support remember-me auto-login here.
        return None
    session = SESSIONS.get(cookie)
    if not session:
        return None
    if is_session_expired(session):
        destroy_session(session.id)
        return None
    touch_session(session)
    return session.user_id
```
