@app.post("/login", response_model=LoginResponse)
async def login(
    payload: LoginRequest,
    request: Request,
    response: Response,
    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
):
    """
    POST /login
    Body: { "email": "...", "password": "...", "remember_me": bool }
    Response:
      200 { "success": true, "user_id": "..." } + Set-Cookie: __Host-session=...
      401 { "success": false } on invalid credentials (uniform message).
    """

    ip = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
    ip_key = f"login:ip:{ip}"
    user_key = f"login:user:{payload.email.lower()}"

    # Per-IP rate limiting
    if rate_limited(ip_key, LOGIN_PER_IP_LIMIT, LOGIN_PER_IP_WINDOW):
        slog.warn(
            "login_rate_limited_ip",
            extra={"ip": ip},
        )
        # Same 429 response body for all.
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="too_many_attempts",
        )

    # Per-user rate limiting (by email)
    if rate_limited(user_key, LOGIN_PER_USER_LIMIT, LOGIN_PER_USER_WINDOW):
        slog.warn(
            "login_rate_limited_user",
            extra={"ip": ip, "email_prefix": payload.email[:3] + "***"},
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="too_many_attempts",
        )

    start = time.time()
    email_key = payload.email.lower()
    user = USERS.get(email_key)

    # To avoid user enumeration and timing oracle, always run a hash verify,
    # even when user does not exist, using a dummy hash.
    dummy_hash = hash_password("dummy-password-for-timing-only")
    if user:
        pw_ok = verify_password(user.password_hash, payload.password)
        locked = user.locked
        user_id = user.id
    else:
        pw_ok = verify_password(dummy_hash, payload.password)
        locked = False
        user_id = None

    # Uniform error for all invalid states
    if (not user) or locked or (not pw_ok):
        elapsed = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
        slog.info(
            "login_failed",
            extra={
                "email_prefix": payload.email[:3] + "***",
                "ip": ip,
                "duration_ms": elapsed,
                "locked": locked if user else False,
            },
        )
        # Same response for "no such user", "locked", "wrong password"
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="invalid_credentials",
        )

    # Successful login
    elapsed = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)

    # Regenerate session ID on login (session fixation defense).
    session = create_session(user_id)

    # Set session cookie with secure attributes. NOTE: FastAPI itself doesn't
    # enforce TLS; ensure deployment is HTTPS-only with HSTS.
    response.set_cookie(
        key=SESSION_COOKIE_NAME,
        value=session.id,
        httponly=True,
        secure=True,
        samesite="lax",
        path="/",
    )

    # Optional remember-me token
    if payload.remember_me:
        rec, validator = create_remember_token(user_id)
        remember_value = f"{rec.selector}:{validator}"
        sig = hmac.new(
            REMEMBER_TOKEN_HMAC_KEY,
            remember_value.encode("utf-8"),
            hashlib.sha256,
        ).hexdigest()
        cookie_val = remember_value + "." + sig
        response.set_cookie(
            key=REMEMBER_COOKIE_NAME,
            value=cookie_val,
            httponly=True,
            secure=True,
            samesite="lax",
            path="/",
            max_age=int(REMEMBER_TOKEN_LIFETIME.total_seconds()),
        )

    slog.info(
        "login_success",
        extra={
            "user_id": user_id,
            "ip": ip,
            "duration_ms": elapsed,
        },
    )

    return LoginResponse(success=True, user_id=user_id)

def search_documents_for_org(
    org_id: UUID,
    keyword: Optional[str],
    category: Optional[str],
    limit: int,
    offset: int,
) -> SearchResponse:
    keyword_lower = keyword.lower() if keyword else None
    category_lower = category.lower() if category else None

    # Filter by org
    filtered = [d for d in DOCS_DB if d.org_id == org_id]

    # Filter by category
    if category_lower:
        filtered = [d for d in filtered if d.category.lower() == category_lower]

    # Filter by keyword in title or content
    if keyword_lower:
        filtered = [
            d for d in filtered
            if keyword_lower in d.title.lower() or keyword_lower in d.content.lower()
        ]

    total = len(filtered)
    sliced = filtered[offset : offset + limit]

    def make_snippet(doc: Document) -> str:
        text = doc.content
        if not keyword_lower:
            return text[:160] + ("..." if len(text) > 160 else "")
        idx = text.lower().find(keyword_lower)
        if idx == -1:
            return text[:160] + ("..." if len(text) > 160 else "")
        start = max(idx - 40, 0)
        end = min(idx + len(keyword_lower) + 40, len(text))
        snippet = text[start:end]
        if start > 0:
            snippet = "..." + snippet
        if end < len(text):
            snippet = snippet + "..."
        return snippet

    results = [
        DocumentOut(
            id=d.id,
            title=d.title,
            content_snippet=make_snippet(d),
            category=d.category,
            created_at=d.created_at,
        )
        for d in sliced
    ]

    return SearchResponse(total=total, results=results)

):

    if not current_user:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,

)
JWT_PATTERN = re.compile(r"eyJ[\w-]{10,}\.[\w-]{10,}\.[\w-]+")


class RedactingFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        msg = super().format(record)
        # CR/LF escaping (log injection)
        msg = msg.replace("\r", "\\r").replace("\n", "\\n")
        # JWT coarse scrubbing
        msg = JWT_PATTERN.sub("[JWT]", msg)
        return msg

)

    """Redact obvious secret fields in dict."""

    for k, v in d.items():

    request: Request,
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
) -> None:
    key = ("search_documents", user.id)
    now = time.time()
    window_start = now - 60
    timestamps = _rate_store.get(key, [])
    # Drop old entries
    timestamps = [t for t in timestamps if t >= window_start]
    if len(timestamps) >= RATE_LIMIT_PER_MINUTE:

import base64
import json


def encode_cursor(created_at: datetime, doc_id: int, category: Optional[str]) -> str:
    payload = {
        "created_at": created_at.isoformat(),
        "id": doc_id,
        "category": category,
    }
    raw = json.dumps(payload, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8")
    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(raw).decode("ascii")


def decode_cursor(cursor: str) -> Tuple[datetime, int, Optional[str]]:
    try:
        raw = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(cursor.encode("ascii"))
        payload = json.loads(raw.decode("utf-8"))
        created_at = datetime.fromisoformat(payload["created_at"])
        doc_id = int(payload["id"])
        category = payload.get("category")
        return created_at, doc_id, category
    except Exception:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="invalid_cursor",
        )

@app.middleware("http")
async def add_trace_id_and_logging(request: Request, call_next):
    trace_id = request.headers.get("X-Trace-Id") or uuid.uuid4().hex
    start = time.time()
    try:
        response: Response = await call_next(request)
    except HTTPException as exc:
        # Structured error logging
        duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
        evt = {
            "event": "http_request",
            "trace_id": trace_id,
            "method": request.method,
            "path": request.url.path,
            "status": exc.status_code,
            "duration_ms": duration_ms,
            "error_type": "HTTPException",
            "error_detail": exc.detail,
        }
        logger.error(json.dumps(redact_dict(evt)))
        problem = ErrorResponse(
            type=f"https://api.example.com/errors/{exc.detail}",
            title="Request error",
            status=exc.status_code,
            detail=exc.detail,
            trace_id=trace_id,
        )
        return Response(
            content=problem.model_dump_json(),
            media_type="application/problem+json",
            status_code=exc.status_code,
        )

    # Successful or non-HTTPException error
    duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
    evt = {
        "event": "http_request",
        "trace_id": trace_id,
        "method": request.method,
        "path": request.url.path,
        "status": response.status_code,
        "duration_ms": duration_ms,
    }
    logger.info(json.dumps(redact_dict(evt)))
    response.headers["X-Trace-Id"] = trace_id
    return response


@app.exception_handler(Exception)
async def unhandled_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception):
    trace_id = uuid.uuid4().hex
    evt = {
        "event": "unhandled_exception",
        "trace_id": trace_id,
        "path": request.url.path,
        "error_type": exc.__class__.__name__,
    }
    logger.error(json.dumps(redact_dict(evt)))
    problem = ErrorResponse(
        type="https://api.example.com/errors/internal",
        title="Internal Server Error",
        status=500,
        detail="internal_error",
        trace_id=trace_id,
    )
    return Response(
        content=problem.model_dump_json(),
        media_type="application/problem+json",
        status_code=500,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# INPUT VALIDATION HELPERS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MAX_KEYWORD_LEN = 128
MAX_CATEGORY_LEN = 64
MAX_LIMIT = 50


def validate_search_params(
    keyword: Optional[str],
    category: Optional[str],
    limit: int,
) -> None:
    if keyword is not None:
        keyword = keyword.strip()
        if not keyword:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="keyword_empty")
        if len(keyword) > MAX_KEYWORD_LEN:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="keyword_too_long")

    if category is not None:
        category = category.strip()
        if not category:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="category_empty")
        if len(category) > MAX_CATEGORY_LEN:
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="category_too_long")

    if limit < 1 or limit > MAX_LIMIT:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="invalid_limit")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEARCH ENDPOINT
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get(
    "/documents/search",
    response_model=SearchResponse,
    summary="Search documents by keyword and category within caller's organization",
)
def search_documents(
    keyword: Optional[str] = Query(
        default=None,
        description="Keyword to search in title and content",
        max_length=MAX_KEYWORD_LEN,
    ),
    category: Optional[str] = Query(
        default=None,
        description="Exact category filter",
        max_length=MAX_CATEGORY_LEN,
    ),
    limit: int = Query(
        default=20,
        ge=1,
        le=MAX_LIMIT,
        description="Max number of items to return (1-50)",
    ),
    cursor: Optional[str] = Query(
        default=None,
        description="Opaque cursor for pagination",
    ),
    user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
    _: None = Depends(rate_limiter),
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    """
    Search documents scoped to the authenticated user's organization.

    - Full-text-lite: case-insensitive `LIKE` on title and content
      (for real search, use full-text index).
    - Cursor-based pagination: stable by (created_at DESC, id DESC).
    - Organization (tenant) scoping enforced in the query.
    """
    validate_search_params(keyword, category, limit)

    # Build base predicate: tenant isolation
    conditions = [Document.organization_id == user.organization_id]

    if keyword:
        kw = f"%{keyword.strip()}%"
        conditions.append(
            or_(
                Document.title.ilike(kw),
                Document.content.ilike(kw),
            )
        )
    if category:
        conditions.append(Document.category == category.strip())

    # Cursor handling
    if cursor:
        cur_created_at, cur_id, cur_category = decode_cursor(cursor)
        # ensure cursor is consistent with category filter
        if (cur_category or None) != (category or None):
            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="cursor_mismatch")
        # Seek semantics: items strictly before the cursor
        conditions.append(
            or_(
                Document.created_at < cur_created_at,
                and_(
                    Document.created_at == cur_created_at,
                    Document.id < cur_id,
                ),
            )
        )

    stmt = (
        select(Document)
        .where(and_(*conditions))
        .order_by(Document.created_at.desc(), Document.id.desc())
        .limit(limit + 1)  # over-fetch by 1 to know has_more
    )

    results = db.execute(stmt).scalars().all()
    has_more = len(results) > limit
    docs = results[:limit]

    next_cursor = None
    if has_more:
        last = docs[-1]
        next_cursor = encode_cursor(last.created_at, last.id, category)

    out_docs = [
        DocumentOut(
            id=d.id,
            title=d.title,
            category=d.category,
            created_at=d.created_at,
        )
        for d in docs
    ]

    return SearchResponse(data=out_docs, next_cursor=next_cursor, has_more=has_more)

Notes on the required controls:

- **Abuse control**: `rate_limiter` dependency enforces per-user rate limiting; tests verify it returns 429.
- **Transport enforcement**: This code assumes TLS/HSTS at gateway; document that in your deployment and terminate plaintext only on loopback.
- **Tests**: `test_app.py` exercises auth, scoping, category filter, pagination, cursor, and rate limiting; they assert actual behavior.
- **Structured logging without secrets/PII**:
  - JSON-style events in middleware and exception handler.
  - Redaction pattern for secret-like keys; JWT masking; CR/LF escaping.
  - No passwords/tokens/API keys are logged from inputs.

You can adapt the auth layer (e.g., OAuth2/JWT) and swap SQLite for Postgres with minimal changes while keeping the same security and design patterns.

def verify_signature(raw_body: bytes, signature_header: str, secret: str) -> bool:
    """
    Simple HMAC-based signature verification.
    Actual implementation must match your provider's docs.
    """
    if not signature_header:
        return False

    # Example: provider sends header like: t=timestamp, sig=hex
    # signature_header = "t=1660000000,sig=abc123..."
    parts = dict(p.split("=", 1) for p in signature_header.split(",") if "=" in p)
    sig = parts.get("sig")
    timestamp = parts.get("t")

    if not sig or not timestamp:
        return False

    # Optional: check timestamp to avoid replay attacks
    try:
        ts = int(timestamp)
        if abs(time.time() - ts) > 5 * 60:
            return False
    except ValueError:
        return False

    signed_payload = f"{timestamp}.{raw_body.decode('utf-8')}"
    computed_sig = hmac.new(
        key=secret.encode("utf-8"),
        msg=signed_payload.encode("utf-8"),
        digestmod=hashlib.sha256,
    ).hexdigest()

    # Compare signatures in constant time
    return hmac.compare_digest(computed_sig, sig)

async def handle_payment_webhook(
    request: Request,
    x_payment_signature: Optional[str] = Header(None),
):
    # 1. Read raw body
    raw_body = await request.body()

    if not verify_signature(raw_body, x_payment_signature or "", WEBHOOK_SECRET):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid signature")

    # 3. Parse JSON
    try:
        payload = json.loads(raw_body.decode("utf-8"))
        event = WebhookEvent(**payload)
    except Exception:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid payload")

    # 4. Idempotency: skip already processed events
    if event.id in PROCESSED_EVENTS:
        return JSONResponse(status_code=200, content={"received": True, "idempotent": True})

    # 5. Route by event type
    if event.type == "payment.succeeded":

    elif event.type == "payment.failed":
        await handle_payment_failed(event)

        return JSONResponse(

            content={"received": True, "ignored_event_type": event.type},
        )

    PROCESSED_EVENTS.add(event.id)
    return JSONResponse(status_code=200, content={"received": True})

    order = ORDERS_DB.get(order_id)

    order = ORDERS_DB.get(order_id)

    order["payment_id"] = event.data.id
    order["failed_at"] = event.created_at

"""
FastAPI webhook consumer for a payment provider.

Implements:
- HTTPS-only deployment assumption (no HTTP here; enforced at gateway)
- HMAC-SHA256 signing (Standard Webhooks style: id + "." + ts + "." + raw_body)
- Timestamp-based replay window
- Idempotent processing via event-id dedupe
- Order update logic with status transitions
- Per-endpoint rate limiting (very simple in-memory token bucket)
- Structured JSON logging without secrets/PII
- Tests (pytest) demonstrating signature verification, replay, idempotency, update
"""

import hmac
import hashlib
import json
import logging
import time
from typing import List, Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request, HTTPException, status, Depends
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logging: structured, JSON, no secrets
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        base = {
            "event": getattr(record, "event", record.getMessage().split()[0] if record.getMessage() else "log"),
            "level": record.levelname,
            "timestamp": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime(record.created)),
            "logger": record.name,
        }
        # Merge extra attrs except built-ins
        for k, v in record.__dict__.items():
            if k in base or k.startswith("_"):
                continue
            if k in ("msg", "args", "levelname", "levelno", "pathname", "filename",
                     "module", "exc_info", "exc_text", "stack_info", "lineno",
                     "funcName", "created", "msecs", "relativeCreated", "thread",
                     "threadName", "processName", "process"):
                continue
            base[k] = v
        return json.dumps(base, default=str)


logger = logging.getLogger("webhooks")
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
handler = logging.StreamHandler()
handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
logger.handlers = [handler]
logger.propagate = False


def log_event(event: str, level: int = logging.INFO, **fields):
    logger.log(level, event, extra={"event": event, **fields})


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Very simple in-memory "DB" for demo purposes
# (Replace with real persistent storage in production.)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class OrderStatus(str):
    PENDING = "pending"
    PAID = "paid"
    FAILED = "failed"
    CANCELED = "canceled"


class Order(BaseModel):
    id: str
    amount_cents: int
    currency: str
    status: str = Field(default=OrderStatus.PENDING)
    payment_provider_charge_id: Optional[str] = None
    updated_at: float = Field(default_factory=lambda: time.time())


ORDERS: dict[str, Order] = {}
PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS: set[str] = set()  # idempotency key storage (demo only)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rate limiting (very simple, per-remote host token bucket in memory)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS = 60  # max
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SEC = 60


class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, capacity: int, window_seconds: int):
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.window = window_seconds
        self._buckets: dict[str, list[float]] = {}

    def allow(self, key: str) -> bool:
        now = time.time()
        bucket = self._buckets.get(key, [])
        # drop old tokens
        bucket = [ts for ts in bucket if now - ts < self.window]
        if len(bucket) >= self.capacity:
            self._buckets[key] = bucket
            return False
        bucket.append(now)
        self._buckets[key] = bucket
        return True


rate_limiter = RateLimiter(RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SEC)


async def rate_limit_dependency(request: Request):
    # Use client hostname/IP as key (behind a real gateway use a more stable tenant key)
    client_host = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
    if not rate_limiter.allow(client_host):
        log_event(
            "webhook_rate_limited",
            logging.WARN,
            client_host=client_host,
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="Too many webhook requests",
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Webhook signature verification
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# In a real app: retrieve from a secret manager, allow multiple active secrets.
ACTIVE_WEBHOOK_SECRETS: List[bytes] = [
    b"super-secret-demo-key-change-me",  # demo only
]

WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER = "webhook-id"
WEBHOOK_TIMESTAMP_HEADER = "webhook-timestamp"
WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_HEADER = "webhook-signature"
WEBHOOK_TOLERANCE_SECONDS = 300  # ±5 minutes


def _parse_signature_header(header_val: str) -> List[tuple[str, bytes]]:
    """
    Parse header like: "v1,BASE64SIG1;v1,BASE64SIG2"
    Returns list of (version, raw_bytes).
    """
    sigs: List[tuple[str, bytes]] = []
    for part in header_val.split(";"):
        part = part.strip()
        if not part:
            continue
        try:
            version, b64sig = part.split(",", 1)
        except ValueError:
            continue
        try:
            sig_bytes = base64_decode(b64sig)
        except Exception:
            continue
        sigs.append((version, sig_bytes))
    return sigs


def base64_decode(s: str) -> bytes:
    import base64
    return base64.b64decode(s, validate=True)


def base64_encode(b: bytes) -> str:
    import base64
    return base64.b64encode(b).decode("ascii")


async def verify_webhook_signature(request: Request) -> bytes:
    """
    Dependency that:
    - reads raw body bytes once
    - enforces timestamp tolerance
    - verifies HMAC-SHA256 over id + "." + ts + "." + body
    Returns raw body bytes if verification passes.
    """
    raw_body = await request.body()

    event_id = request.headers.get(WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER)
    ts_header = request.headers.get(WEBHOOK_TIMESTAMP_HEADER)
    sig_header = request.headers.get(WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_HEADER)

    if not event_id or not ts_header or not sig_header:
        log_event(
            "webhook_missing_headers",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Missing webhook authentication headers",
        )

    try:
        ts_int = int(ts_header)
    except ValueError:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Invalid webhook timestamp",
        )

    now = int(time.time())
    if abs(now - ts_int) > WEBHOOK_TOLERANCE_SECONDS:
        log_event(
            "webhook_timestamp_out_of_range",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
            webhook_timestamp=ts_int,
            now=now,
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Webhook timestamp outside allowed window",
        )

    try:
        parsed_sigs = _parse_signature_header(sig_header)
    except Exception:
        parsed_sigs = []

    if not parsed_sigs:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid webhook signature header",
        )

    message = f"{event_id}.{ts_int}.".encode("utf-8") + raw_body
    verified = False

    for secret in ACTIVE_WEBHOOK_SECRETS:
        digest = hmac.new(secret, message, hashlib.sha256).digest()
        for version, sig_bytes in parsed_sigs:
            if version != "v1":
                continue
            if hmac.compare_digest(digest, sig_bytes):
                verified = True
                break
        if verified:
            break

    if not verified:
        log_event(
            "webhook_signature_invalid",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid webhook signature",
        )

    # log auth success (no body, no secrets)
    log_event(
        "webhook_signature_verified",
        logging.INFO,
        event_id=event_id,
    )

    return raw_body


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pydantic models for webhook payload
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class WebhookPaymentObject(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    id: str
    order_id: str
    amount_cents: int
    currency: str
    status: str  # "succeeded" | "failed" | "canceled" etc.

    type: str
    created_at: float
    api_version: str
    data: dict

app = FastAPI(title="Payment Webhook Consumer")


class LoggingMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        start = time.time()
        try:
            response = await call_next(request)
            status_code = response.status_code
            outcome = "success" if 200 <= status_code < 300 else "error"
            log_event(
                "http_request",
                logging.INFO,
                method=request.method,
                path=request.url.path,
                status_code=status_code,
                duration_ms=int((time.time() - start) * 1000),
                outcome=outcome,
            )
            return response
        except Exception as exc:
            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            log_event(
                "http_request_error",
                logging.ERROR,
                method=request.method,
                path=request.url.path,
                duration_ms=duration_ms,
                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
            )
            raise


app.add_middleware(LoggingMiddleware)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Webhook endpoint
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.post("/webhooks/payment")
async def payment_webhook(
    request: Request,
    _: None = Depends(rate_limit_dependency),  # abuse control
    raw_body: bytes = Depends(verify_webhook_signature),
):
    """
    Receive a payment provider webhook and update the corresponding order.
    """
    event_id = request.headers[WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER]

    # Idempotency: dedupe by event id
    if event_id in PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS:
        log_event(
            "webhook_duplicate_ignored",
            logging.INFO,
            event_id=event_id,
        )
        # Return 200 so provider stops retrying
        return JSONResponse({"status": "ok", "idempotent": True})

    try:
        envelope = WebhookEnvelope.model_validate_json(raw_body)
    except Exception as e:
        log_event(
            "webhook_payload_invalid",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
            error=str(e),
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Invalid webhook payload",
        )

    # For this example, expect a payment-related event type
    if envelope.type not in {"payment.succeeded", "payment.failed", "payment.canceled"}:
        log_event(
            "webhook_event_type_ignored",
            logging.INFO,
            event_id=event_id,
            event_type=envelope.type,
        )
        PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.add(event_id)
        return JSONResponse({"status": "ignored", "reason": "unsupported_type"})

    try:
        payment_obj = WebhookPaymentObject.model_validate(envelope.data.get("object", {}))
    except Exception as e:
        log_event(
            "webhook_payment_object_invalid",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
            error=str(e),
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Invalid payment object",
        )

    order = ORDERS.get(payment_obj.order_id)
    if not order:
        log_event(
            "webhook_order_not_found",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
            order_id=payment_obj.order_id,
        )
        # Still mark as processed to avoid retry storm on unknown orders
        PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.add(event_id)
        return JSONResponse(
            {"status": "ignored", "reason": "order_not_found"},
            status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED,
        )

    # Basic semantic validation: match amount/currency
    if (
        order.amount_cents != payment_obj.amount_cents
        or order.currency.lower() != payment_obj.currency.lower()
    ):
        log_event(
            "webhook_amount_mismatch",
            logging.WARN,
            event_id=event_id,
            order_id=order.id,
            order_amount=order.amount_cents,
            payment_amount=payment_obj.amount_cents,
            order_currency=order.currency,
            payment_currency=payment_obj.currency,
        )
        PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.add(event_id)
        return JSONResponse(
            {"status": "ignored", "reason": "amount_mismatch"},
            status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED,
        )

    # Apply state transition
    prev_status = order.status
    if envelope.type == "payment.succeeded":
        order.status = OrderStatus.PAID
    elif envelope.type == "payment.failed":
        order.status = OrderStatus.FAILED
    elif envelope.type == "payment.canceled":
        order.status = OrderStatus.CANCELED

    order.payment_provider_charge_id = payment_obj.id
    order.updated_at = time.time()
    ORDERS[order.id] = order
    PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.add(event_id)

    log_event(
        "order_updated_from_webhook",
        logging.INFO,
        event_id=event_id,
        order_id=order.id,
        previous_status=prev_status,
        new_status=order.status,
        payment_id=payment_obj.id,
        event_type=envelope.type,
    )

    return JSONResponse({"status": "ok"})

# Test support helpers (used by pytest)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def sign_webhook(
    secret: bytes,
    event_id: str,
    timestamp: int,
    body: bytes,
    version: str = "v1",
) -> str:
    msg = f"{event_id}.{timestamp}.".encode("utf-8") + body
    digest = hmac.new(secret, msg, hashlib.sha256).digest()
    return f"{version},{base64_encode(digest)}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Basic tests (pytest)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Place the following in a separate test file when using this in a real project.

def _build_sample_order():

        "type": f"payment.{status}",

        "api_version": "2026-06-01",

            "object": {

                "amount_cents": 1000,
                "currency": "USD",

    }
    return json.dumps(env).encode("utf-8")


def test_signature_and_update():
    from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

    client = TestClient(app)
    ORDERS.clear()
    PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.clear()

    order = _build_sample_order()
    body = _build_sample_webhook_body(order.id, "succeeded")
    ts = int(time.time())
    event_id = "evt_01TEST"
    sig_header = sign_webhook(ACTIVE_WEBHOOK_SECRETS[0], event_id, ts, body)

    r = client.post(
        "/webhooks/payment",
        data=body,
        headers={
            WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER: event_id,
            WEBHOOK_TIMESTAMP_HEADER: str(ts),
            WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_HEADER: sig_header,

    )

    assert ORDERS[order.id].status == OrderStatus.PAID


def test_replay_rejected_by_timestamp():
    from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

    client = TestClient(app)
    ORDERS.clear()
    PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.clear()

    order = _build_sample_order()
    body = _build_sample_webhook_body(order.id, "succeeded")
    # old timestamp
    ts = int(time.time()) - (WEBHOOK_TOLERANCE_SECONDS + 10)
    event_id = "evt_OLD"
    sig_header = sign_webhook(ACTIVE_WEBHOOK_SECRETS[0], event_id, ts, body)

    r = client.post(
        "/webhooks/payment",
        data=body,
        headers={
            WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER: event_id,
            WEBHOOK_TIMESTAMP_HEADER: str(ts),
            WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_HEADER: sig_header,
        },
    )
    assert r.status_code == 400
    # order not updated
    assert ORDERS[order.id].status == OrderStatus.PENDING


def test_idempotent_duplicate_ignored():
    from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

    client = TestClient(app)
    ORDERS.clear()
    PROCESSED_WEBHOOK_IDS.clear()

    order = _build_sample_order()
    body = _build_sample_webhook_body(order.id, "succeeded")
    ts = int(time.time())
    event_id = "evt_IDEMPOTENT"
    sig_header = sign_webhook(ACTIVE_WEBHOOK_SECRETS[0], event_id, ts, body)

    # first call
    r1 = client.post(
        "/webhooks/payment",
        data=body,
        headers={
            WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER: event_id,
            WEBHOOK_TIMESTAMP_HEADER: str(ts),
            WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_HEADER: sig_header,
        },
    )
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    assert ORDERS[order.id].status == OrderStatus.PAID

    # second call (duplicate)
    r2 = client.post(
        "/webhooks/payment",
        data=body,
        headers={
            WEBHOOK_ID_HEADER: event_id,
            WEBHOOK_TIMESTAMP_HEADER: str(ts),
            WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_HEADER: sig_header,
        },
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json().get("idempotent") is True
    # status unchanged
    assert ORDERS[order.id].status == OrderStatus.PAID

Notes on the required controls:

- Abuse control: `rate_limit_dependency` with a simple in-memory token bucket keyed by client host; in production you’d implement this at gateway or via Redis/sidecar.
- Transport enforcement: this code is written for FastAPI behind an HTTPS-only ingress with HSTS; configure TLS at your reverse proxy (e.g. nginx, cloud LB). No HTTP listener is configured here.
- Tests: three pytest-style tests at the bottom validate signature verification, replay window enforcement, idempotent handling, and order status updates. To run them, extract this file into a module and run `pytest`.
- Structured logging: `JsonFormatter` produces one-line JSON events; logging calls avoid payload bodies and secrets and include clear event names and fields.

from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException, status, Body
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordRequestForm
from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr
from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Boolean, DateTime
from sqlalchemy.orm import declarative_base, sessionmaker, Session
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from passlib.context import CryptContext
import jwt

# ---------------------------
# CONFIG / SETUP
# ---------------------------

DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./test.db"
SECRET_KEY = "CHANGE_THIS_TO_A_SECURE_RANDOM_KEY"
ALGORITHM = "HS256"
RESET_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 60

pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")

engine = create_engine(
    DATABASE_URL, connect_args={"check_same_thread": False}
)
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)
Base = declarative_base()

app = FastAPI()


# ---------------------------
# DATABASE MODELS
# ---------------------------

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True, nullable=False)
    hashed_password = Column(String, nullable=False)
    is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)


Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)


# ---------------------------
# UTILS
# ---------------------------

def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()


def get_user_by_email(db: Session, email: str) -> Optional[User]:
    return db.query(User).filter(User.email == email).first()


def verify_password(plain_password: str, hashed_password: str) -> bool:
    return pwd_context.verify(plain_password, hashed_password)


def get_password_hash(password: str) -> str:
    return pwd_context.hash(password)


def create_reset_token(user_id: int) -> str:
    expire = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=RESET_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)
    payload = {"sub": str(user_id), "exp": expire}
    return jwt.encode(payload, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)


def verify_reset_token(token: str) -> int:
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
        user_id = payload.get("sub")

            raise HTTPException(
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
                detail="Invalid token",
            )

    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:

            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Token has expired",

    except jwt.PyJWTError:

@app.post("/register", response_model=UserOut)

            detail="Email already registered",
        )
    user = User(
        email=user_in.email,
        hashed_password=get_password_hash(user_in.password),
    )
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(user)
    return user


# ---------------------------
# FORGOT PASSWORD FLOW

@app.post("/forgot-password", status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK)
def forgot_password(data: PasswordResetRequest, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    """
    Step 1: User submits email, we generate a reset token and email the link.
    Response is always 200 (if user exists or not) to avoid account enumeration.
    """
    user = get_user_by_email(db, data.email)
    if user:
        token = create_reset_token(user.id)
        send_password_reset_email(user.email, token)
    # Always respond OK
    return {"message": "If this email exists, a reset link has been sent."}


@app.post("/reset-password", status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK)
def reset_password(data: PasswordResetConfirm, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    """
    Step 2: User clicks link from email (containing the token) and submits new password.
    """
    user_id = verify_reset_token(data.token)
    user = db.query(User).filter(User.id == user_id).first()
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="User not found",
        )
    user.hashed_password = get_password_hash(data.new_password)
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    return {"message": "Password has been reset successfully."}

import hashlib
import hmac
import logging
import secrets
import string
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Optional, Dict

from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException, status, Request, BackgroundTasks
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr, Field
from argon2 import PasswordHasher, exceptions as argon2_exceptions
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

# ============================================================
# CONFIG / CONSTANTS
# ============================================================

RESET_TOKEN_TTL = timedelta(hours=1)         # ≤1h expiry (rule 5)
PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH = 15                     # NIST 800-63B for single-factor
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW = timedelta(minutes=1)
RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_IP = 5           # reset-request abuse control
RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_EMAIL = 3

# TLS/HSTS are enforced at gateway / reverse proxy:
# - Only HTTPS ingress is allowed
# - HSTS header (Strict-Transport-Security) set at the edge
# This service assumes it is only reachable over TLS and does not expose HTTP.

# ============================================================
# LOGGER: structured JSON, redaction for secrets/PII
# ============================================================

logger = logging.getLogger("password_reset")
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)

handler = logging.StreamHandler()
handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)

SECRET_KEYS = hashlib.sha256(b"secret-key-patterns").hexdigest()  # dummy, not used directly

class JsonFormatter(logging.Formatter):
    def format(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> str:
        # Basic structured log; keep minimal but machine-parseable.
        payload = {
            "event": getattr(record, "event", record.getMessage().split(" ")[0]),
            "level": record.levelname,
            "message": record.getMessage(),
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }
        # Attach extra structured fields if present
        for attr in ("client_ip", "route", "status_code", "user_id", "email_hash", "cid"):
            val = getattr(record, attr, None)
            if val is not None:
                payload[attr] = val

        # Basic CR/LF sanitization for log injection prevention
        for k, v in list(payload.items()):
            if isinstance(v, str):
                payload[k] = v.replace("\r", "\\r").replace("\n", "\\n")
        import json
        return json.dumps(payload)

handler.setFormatter(JsonFormatter())
logger.addHandler(handler)
logger.propagate = False

# ============================================================
# RATE LIMITING (simple in-memory; use Redis in production)
# ============================================================

class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self):
        # key -> [timestamps]
        self.by_ip: Dict[str, list[datetime]] = {}
        self.by_email: Dict[str, list[datetime]] = {}

    def _prune(self, timestamps: list[datetime], now: datetime) -> list[datetime]:
        cutoff = now - RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW
        return [t for t in timestamps if t > cutoff]

    def check_ip(self, ip: str, now: datetime) -> bool:
        ts = self.by_ip.get(ip, [])
        ts = self._prune(ts, now)
        self.by_ip[ip] = ts
        if len(ts) >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_IP:
            return False
        ts.append(now)
        return True

    def check_email(self, email: str, now: datetime) -> bool:
        key = email.lower()
        ts = self.by_email.get(key, [])
        ts = self._prune(ts, now)
        self.by_email[key] = ts
        if len(ts) >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS_PER_EMAIL:
            return False
        ts.append(now)
        return True

rate_limiter = RateLimiter()

def get_client_ip(request: Request) -> str:
    # In production, trust only a sanitized, gateway-set header (e.g. X-Real-IP)
    # and strip user-provided headers at the edge. Here we use client.host.
    return request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"

# ============================================================
# CRYPTO / PASSWORD HASHER
# ============================================================

ph = PasswordHasher(time_cost=3, memory_cost=65536, parallelism=4)  # argon2id defaults (rule 02)

def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
    return ph.hash(password)

def verify_password(hashed: str, password: str) -> bool:
    try:
        return ph.verify(hashed, password)
    except argon2_exceptions.VerifyMismatchError:
        return False

# Single secret pepper is optional; in real deployment, store outside DB (KMS/env).
PEPPER: Optional[bytes] = None  # For demo, not used; add if you can store securely.

def hash_reset_token(token: str) -> str:
    # Reset tokens are high-entropy, so SHA-256 is acceptable (rule 04).
    # Store only hash at rest.
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    digest.update(token.encode("utf-8"))
    return digest.hexdigest()

def constant_time_equal(a: str, b: str) -> bool:
    # Compare secrets constant-time (rule 04 §6)
    return hmac.compare_digest(a.encode("utf-8"), b.encode("utf-8"))

# ============================================================
# IN-MEMORY "DB" MODELS
# ============================================================

class User:
    def __init__(self, user_id: int, email: str, password_hash: str):
        self.id = user_id
        self.email = email
        self.password_hash = password_hash

class PasswordResetToken:
    def __init__(self, user_id: int, token_hash: str, expires_at: datetime, used: bool = False):
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.token_hash = token_hash
        self.expires_at = expires_at
        self.used = used

# very simple in-memory stores
USERS_BY_EMAIL: Dict[str, User] = {}
USERS_BY_ID: Dict[int, User] = {}
RESET_TOKENS: Dict[str, PasswordResetToken] = {}  # key: token_hash

def get_user_by_email(email: str) -> Optional[User]:
    return USERS_BY_EMAIL.get(email.lower())

def get_user_by_id(user_id: int) -> Optional[User]:
    return USERS_BY_ID.get(user_id)

def store_user(user: User) -> None:
    USERS_BY_ID[user.id] = user
    USERS_BY_EMAIL[user.email.lower()] = user

def create_reset_token(user_id: int) -> str:
    raw = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)  # ~256 bits entropy (rule 04)
    hashed = hash_reset_token(raw)
    expires = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + RESET_TOKEN_TTL
    # Invalidate any existing tokens for this user
    for key, t in list(RESET_TOKENS.items()):
        if t.user_id == user_id:
            del RESET_TOKENS[key]
    RESET_TOKENS[hashed] = PasswordResetToken(user_id=user_id, token_hash=hashed, expires_at=expires)
    return raw

def consume_reset_token(raw_token: str) -> Optional[PasswordResetToken]:
    hashed = hash_reset_token(raw_token)
    token_obj = RESET_TOKENS.get(hashed)
    if token_obj is None:
        return None
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    if token_obj.used or token_obj.expires_at < now:
        # Cleanup expired/used
        del RESET_TOKENS[hashed]
        return None
    # single-use: mark used and delete
    token_obj.used = True
    del RESET_TOKENS[hashed]
    return token_obj

# EMAIL SENDER (stub)
# ============================================================

    logger.info(
        "password_reset_email_queued",

    )

# ============================================================
# FASTAPI APP & MIDDLEWARE

app = FastAPI(title="Password Reset Backend", version="1.0.0")

# CORS: Allowlist configured elsewhere as needed; no credentials for this demo.
app.add_middleware(

class CorrelationIdMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        # Simple correlation ID; in real stack, use W3C traceparent / OpenTelemetry.
        cid = secrets.token_hex(16)
        request.state.correlation_id = cid
        try:
            response = await call_next(request)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(
                "unhandled_exception",
                extra={
                    "event": "unhandled_exception",
                    "cid": cid,
                    "route": request.url.path,
                    "client_ip": get_client_ip(request),
                },
            )
            return JSONResponse(
                status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
                content={"error": "internal_error", "cid": cid},
            )
        response.headers["X-Correlation-ID"] = cid
        return response

app.add_middleware(CorrelationIdMiddleware)

# SCHEMAS

class ForgotPasswordRequest(BaseModel):
    email: EmailStr

class ResetPasswordRequest(BaseModel):

    new_password: str = Field(..., min_length=PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH)

# ============================================================
# SESSION INVALIDATION STUB
# ============================================================

def revoke_all_sessions_for_user(user_id: int) -> None:
    # In real app, this would:
    # - Delete all server-side sessions for user
    # - Revoke/denylist any refresh tokens
    # Here we only log a structured event.
    logger.info(
        "sessions_revoked_on_password_change",
        extra={"event": "sessions_revoked_on_password_change", "user_id": user_id},
    )

# ============================================================
# ENDPOINTS

@app.post("/auth/forgot-password", status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED)
async def forgot_password(
    payload: ForgotPasswordRequest,
    request: Request,
    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
):
    """
    Request a password reset email.
    - Always respond 202 to avoid account enumeration.
    - Rate-limited per IP and per email.
    """
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    client_ip = get_client_ip(request)
    cid = getattr(request.state, "correlation_id", None)

    # Rate-limit by IP first
    if not rate_limiter.check_ip(client_ip, now):
        logger.warning(
            "forgot_password_rate_limited_ip",
            extra={
                "event": "forgot_password_rate_limited_ip",
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "cid": cid,
            },
        )
        # 429 here is acceptable: does not reveal if user exists.
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="rate_limited",
        )

    email = payload.email
    email_hash = hashlib.sha256(email.lower().encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()

    # Rate-limit by email (to prevent SMS/email bombing).
    if not rate_limiter.check_email(email, now):
        logger.warning(
            "forgot_password_rate_limited_email",
            extra={
                "event": "forgot_password_rate_limited_email",
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "email_hash": email_hash,
                "cid": cid,
            },
        )
        # Still generic to caller.
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            detail="rate_limited",
        )

    user = get_user_by_email(email)
    if user:
        # Create token and send email in background without exposing existence to client.
        raw_token = create_reset_token(user_id=user.id)
        # You would construct a URL pointing to your frontend reset page.
        reset_link = f"https://app.example.com/reset-password?token={raw_token}"
        background_tasks.add_task(send_password_reset_email, email, reset_link)

        logger.info(
            "forgot_password_requested_existing_user",
            extra={
                "event": "forgot_password_requested_existing_user",
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "email_hash": email_hash,
                "cid": cid,
            },
        )
    else:
        # No such user: log only anonymized hash; client still sees 202.
        logger.info(
            "forgot_password_requested_nonexistent_user",
            extra={
                "event": "forgot_password_requested_nonexistent_user",
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "email_hash": email_hash,
                "cid": cid,
            },
        )

    # Always return the same response to avoid enumeration.
    return {"message": "if an account exists, a reset email has been sent"}

@app.post("/auth/reset-password", status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK)
async def reset_password(
    payload: ResetPasswordRequest,
    request: Request,
):
    """
    Reset password using the emailed token.
    - Token is single-use, hashed at rest, 1h TTL.
    - On success, changes password and revokes all sessions.
    """
    cid = getattr(request.state, "correlation_id", None)
    client_ip = get_client_ip(request)

    # Basic password policy enforcement.
    if len(payload.new_password) < PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="password_too_short",
        )

    token_obj = consume_reset_token(payload.token)
    if not token_obj:
        # Do not reveal if token was invalid vs expired vs already used.
        logger.warning(
            "reset_password_invalid_or_expired_token",
            extra={
                "event": "reset_password_invalid_or_expired_token",
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "cid": cid,
            },
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="invalid_or_expired_token",
        )

    user = get_user_by_id(token_obj.user_id)
    if not user:
        # Extremely unlikely unless DB is inconsistent; still treat as generic error.
        logger.error(
            "reset_password_user_missing_for_token",
            extra={
                "event": "reset_password_user_missing_for_token",
                "client_ip": client_ip,
                "cid": cid,
            },
        )
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="invalid_or_expired_token",
        )

    # Update password hash
    user.password_hash = hash_password(payload.new_password)
    store_user(user)  # persist in our "DB"

    # Revoke all existing sessions for the user
    revoke_all_sessions_for_user(user.id)

    logger.info(
        "password_reset_success",
        extra={
            "event": "password_reset_success",
            "user_id": user.id,
            "client_ip": client_ip,
            "cid": cid,
        },
    )

    return {"message": "password_reset_successful"}

### Notes on verification (“done with evidence”)

To actually run and verify:

```bash
pip install fastapi uvicorn argon2-cffi pydantic pytest
uvicorn main:app --reload
pytest -q
```

- The FastAPI app starts under Uvicorn; TLS/HSTS must be terminated in front (e.g., nginx/ALB).
- `pytest` runs the tests above. They validate:
  - Generic response for nonexistent users
  - Token creation side-effect for existing users
  - Rate limiting behavior
  - Password policy enforcement

You can extend tests to assert password change by:
- Exposing a login endpoint in tests only, or
- Accessing the in-memory user and checking `verify_password` against the new password after calling `/auth/reset-password` with a real token (by instrumenting `create_reset_token` to be test-aware).
